name: Vigo — cycle to Parque Forestal de Vixiador
name_original: Vigo — circulo al Parque Forestal de Vixiador
region_intro: Jogging around Redondela offers diverse landscapes, blending coastal beauty with lush inland areas. The region features scenic paths along the Ría de Vigo, including the promenade at Cesantes beach, and trails that ascend to viewpoints like Alto da Cabaleira. Inland, runners can explore verdant forests and follow riverside routes such as the Río Tomeza Riverside Trail, which provides varied terrain through woodlands and along waterways. The area's connection to the Camino de Santiago also provides numerous well-marked paths suitable for running. Best jogging routes around Redondela The most popular jogging route is Vigo — cycle to Parque Forestal de Vixiador, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 16 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a blend of urban and natural scenery leading towards a forest park. Another top favourite among local runners is View of Vigo at Sunset – Panoramic view of Vigo loop from A Igrexa, a difficult 11.8 miles (19.0 km) path. This route provides expansive views of Vigo Bay and the surrounding landscape, particularly rewarding during sunset. Local runners also love the Monument to Jules Verne – View of Vigo at Sunset loop from Cabanas, a 18.0 miles (28.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 21 minutes. Jogging around Redondela is defined by coastal paths, lush forests, and scenic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate runs to challenging running trails with significant climbs. The routes in Redondela are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore Redondela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Redondela?
A: Redondela offers a diverse selection of over 19 running routes, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These include paths along the coast, through lush forests, and up to scenic viewpoints.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Redondela's running trails?
A: You'll find a varied terrain in Redondela. Coastal paths offer flat stretches with sea views, while inland routes feature verdant forests, riverside trails like the Río Tomeza, and ascents to viewpoints. Some trails, especially those associated with the Camino de Santiago, can be a mix of urban and natural surfaces, including softer earth paths.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Redondela?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, Redondela does offer options suitable for beginners. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984237178/vigo-cycle-to-parque-forestal-de-vixiador" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vigo — cycle to Parque Forestal de Vixiador&lt;/a&gt; route is a moderate 9.5 km trail that blends urban and natural scenery. The Río Tomeza Riverside Trail is also known for its linear, refreshing experience through forests, often on softer ground.

Q: Can I find challenging running trails in Redondela?
A: Yes, Redondela is home to several challenging running trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20164515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Vigo at Sunset – Panoramic view of Vigo loop from A Igrexa&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 19.0 km path offering expansive views. The region also hosts a 'Backyard Ultra' event, indicating the presence of demanding trail running circuits with significant climbs.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Redondela?
A: Yes, Redondela features several circular running routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40351991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Redondela&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 37.7 km trail that takes you through varied terrain. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31462617" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colosseum – View of Vigo at Sunset loop from Redondela&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.7 km option.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see while jogging in Redondela?
A: Redondela's routes offer stunning natural landmarks and viewpoints. You can jog along Vigo Bay, enjoy views from the promenade at Cesantes beach, or ascend to lookouts like Alto da Cabaleira for panoramic vistas of the Island of San Simón and Vigo Bay. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/566061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall on the Senda del Agua&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/921667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza da Moura&lt;/a&gt; are also beautiful natural spots to discover.

Q: Are Redondela's running trails dog-friendly?
A: Many natural trails in Redondela are suitable for running with dogs, especially those through forests and along rivers. However, specific regulations may apply in certain protected areas or on popular promenades. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash and check local signage.

Q: Are there family-friendly running paths in Redondela?
A: Yes, Redondela offers family-friendly options. The Río Tomeza Riverside Trail, with its linear path through forests and over wooden walkways, provides a pleasant and accessible experience for families. The promenade at Cesantes beach is also a great, flat option for a family jog with beautiful coastal views.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Redondela?
A: The mild, Atlantic-influenced climate of Redondela makes it pleasant for outdoor activities year-round. Spring and early summer bring lush greenery, while autumn offers ideal temperatures for exploring trails. Winters are generally mild, allowing for running, though some trails might be wetter.

Q: Are there any running routes with cafes or pubs nearby in Redondela?
A: Given Redondela's role as a crucial meeting point on the Camino de Santiago, many routes, especially those passing through or near the town center, will have cafes, pubs, and accommodation options readily available. Coastal routes and those near urban areas also tend to have amenities nearby for refreshments.

Q: Is parking available at Redondela's trailheads?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes starting directly in Redondela or nearby towns, public parking is generally accessible. For more remote trails, it's advisable to check specific route details or local information for designated parking areas. Public transport is also an option for accessing some trailheads.

Q: Are permits required for jogging trails in Redondela?
A: Generally, permits are not required for recreational jogging on the established trails and paths in Redondela. The region's focus on outdoor activities, including the Camino de Santiago, means most routes are openly accessible. However, always respect private property and any posted local regulations.
tips: Located in the Parque Forestal del Monte Vixiador, this is a spectacular viewpoint over the Ria de Vigo and Ponte de Rande

Pico San Vicente, at 488 m above sea level, is the highest point of the Vixiador del Monte forest park. It is the Parque Forestal Larges in Vigo that covers an area of almost 185,000 m². Public access recruit park, in Candeán, with trails for jogging, hiking and mountain biking

waypoints.name: View of Ría de Vigo and Rande Bridge from Monte Vixiador
